general_info.definition: Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) synthesized from US anthracite and bituminous coals via oxidation in presence of ultrasonic stimulation

method.conditions: 12 g coal mixed with 200 mL H2O2 (30%), ultra-sonicated at 40 kHz, output power of 700 W for 5-6 h at atmospheric pressure and temperature, then resultant was cooled down to room temperature and neutralized with ammonia.

source: Beneficiated sample of the Blue Gem coal(CS-1)
Beneficiated size fractions of a blend of coal seams(CS-2, CS-3)

quantum_yield.value: 53% for CS1-CQD, 20% for CS2-CQD, and 4% for CS3-CQD.
quantum_yield.reference_material: Quinine sulfate in 0.1 M H₂SO₄ (QY = 54% at 340 nm)

applications.performance: Bright blue fluorescence under UV (365 nm), high water solubility due to oxygen functional groups, stable colloidal solutions.
applications.comparative_advantage: Simple, scalable synthesis from abundant coal resources. It can be considered to be an alternative and feasible method to convert coal into environmentally friendly and high-value CQDs.
